content.text:
<user_query>
Audit .github/workflows for blocking vs non-blocking quality gates. Determine which checks actually block deploy and which don't. Return a release-blocker matrix with CI step names, failure behavior, risk severity, and recommended immediate hardening actions for zero regression.
</user_query>
Exploring `.github/workflows` to map jobs, failure behavior, and what blocks deployment.
Checking for reusable workflows or other CI references:

Here is an audit based only on what is in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\.github\workflows\` (three files). Branch protection and required status checks live in GitHub settings and are not represented in-repo.

---

## Summary: what blocks what

| Pipeline | Trigger | Blocks its own completion? | Blocks Azure app deploy in `deploy.yml`? |
|----------|---------|------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| **`deploy.yml`** (`Build & Deploy BIQc`) | `push` → `main` | Yes, except one job (see below) | This **is** the main Azure deploy |
| **`main_biqc-web.yml`** | `workflow_dispatch` only | All jobs block that run | **No** — separate manual path; does not gate `deploy.yml` |
| **`supabase-functions-deploy.yml`** | `push` → `main` (path filter) or manual | All steps block that workflow | **No** — deploys Edge Functions only; independent of Azure image deploy |

The only explicit **non-blocking** quality gate in YAML is the **`regression-iterations`** job in `deploy.yml`, which sets `continue-on-error: true` (lines 52–55). It also does **not** appear in `needs:` for `build-frontend`, `build-backend`, or `deploy` (those only `needs: [test]`), so full regression failure never stops the Docker build or Azure deploy.

---

## Release-blocker matrix

| CI step / job (name in UI) | Workflow | Failure behavior | Blocks release?* | Risk severity | Immediate hardening (zero-regression posture) |
|----------------------------|----------|------------------|------------------|---------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| **Run Tests** → **Run deploy gate smoke tests** (`pytest … test_deploy_gate_smoke.py`) | `deploy.yml` | Step fails → job fails → builds/deploy do not run | **Yes** | **High** (intended gate) | Keep smoke suite minimal but representative; ensure it covers auth/API contracts you assert in post-deploy smoke; add missing cases if gaps found in incidents. |
| **Iteration Regression (non-blocking)** → **Run full iteration suite** | `deploy.yml` | Job may fail but workflow **succeeds** (`continue-on-error: true`); builds do not wait on this job | **No** | **High** (blind spot) | Remove `continue-on-error` or promote to blocking on `main`; or split: blocking “critical path” subset + nightly full suite with alerts. |
| **Build Frontend** (Docker build/push) | `deploy.yml` | Fails → no deploy | **Yes** | **Medium** | Add `npm ci` + `npm test` / `npm run build` as explicit steps **or** rely on Dockerfile multi-stage build failing on test/lint if you add them there; today there is no separate frontend test job in this file. |
| **Build Backend** (Dockerfile generate + build/push) | `deploy.yml` | Fails → no deploy | **Yes** | **Medium** | Ensure image build runs same Python version/tests as CI; consider `pip check` or import smoke in build stage. |
| **Deploy to Azure** (all substeps: login, container set, restart, image pin verify, curls, hostname, Supabase PATCH, summary) | `deploy.yml` | Any step `exit 1` fails deploy job | **Yes** | **High** (post-deploy safety net) | Fix flaky network steps with retries/timeouts; treat Supabase `curl \| grep` as brittle — validate HTTP status + JSON. |
| **Checkout / Setup / Install** (test & regression jobs) | `deploy.yml` | Standard: fail blocks that job | **Yes** for **test**; regression non-blocking overall | Low | Pin action versions; cache pip where safe. |
| **build** → ACR login, build/push | `main_biqc-web.yml` | Fails manual workflow | **N/A** (manual alternate) | **Medium** | Align tags/secrets with `deploy.yml` to avoid drift; document when to use vs main pipeline. |
| **deploy** → Azure Web App deploy | `main_biqc-web.yml` | Fails manual workflow | **N/A** | **Medium** | No post-deploy smoke in this file — add parity with `deploy.yml` smokes or deprecate path. |
| **Deploy changed edge functions** (Supabase CLI) | `supabase-functions-deploy.yml` | `exit 1` on missing secrets or deploy failure | **Blocks Edge Function deploy only** | **High** for backend-on-Edge | Add dry-run or `supabase functions serve` test job on PR if possible; ensure `index.ts` exists for each function you rely on. |

\* **“Blocks release”** here means: for the **default `main` push path**, would a failure stop shipping the Azure containers (`deploy.yml`). Supabase workflow is a separate release surface.

---

## Evidence (non-blocking regression)

```51:56:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\.github\workflows\deploy.yml
  # ─── NON-BLOCKING ITERATION REGRESSION ────────────────────────────────────
  regression-iterations:
    name: Iteration Regression (non-blocking)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    continue-on-error: true
    needs: [test]
```

Build/deploy dependency chain (regression not in `needs`):

```82:86:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\.github\workflows\deploy.yml
  build-frontend:
    name: Build Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test]
```

```164:168:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\.github\workflows\deploy.yml
  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Azure
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-frontend, build-backend]
```

---

## Gaps worth calling out (not in workflows)

- **No PR workflow** in this folder — nothing here gates merges before `main`; that would be branch protection + optional separate PR workflows.
- **Frontend unit/E2E** is not a named job in `deploy.yml`; risk depends entirely on Docker build and deploy smoke tests.
- **Two Azure frontend deploy mechanisms** (`deploy.yml` vs manual `main_biqc-web.yml`) increase operational drift risk if both are used.

If you want this matrix tied to **GitHub “required checks”** names exactly as shown in branch settings, that has to be read from the repo’s protection rules (not stored in these files).
